title: hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. supracalvum
intro: Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. supracalvum, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family, known for its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Hawkweed can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
